subject: math
topic_title: Frustums, Angle Sum of a Triangle and Exterior Angle
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Calculate volumes of composite solids (combinations of basic shapes)
Calculate volumes of frustums of cones and pyramids
Prove that the angle sum of a triangle is 180°
Apply the exterior angle theorem of a triangle

sections.heading: Composite Solids
sections.content: A composite solid is formed by joining two or more basic solids. Its volume is the sum of the individual volumes. Example: a cube of edge 28cm topped by a square pyramid of height 16cm → Volume = (1/3) × 28² × 16 + 28³ = (12544/3) + 21952 ≈ 26133cm³.

sections.heading: Volumes of Frustums
sections.content: A frustum is the part of a cone or pyramid left after removing the top portion with a cut parallel to the base. Volume of frustum = Volume of full solid − Volume of the removed top portion. To find dimensions of the removed part, use similar triangles (the linear scale factor equals the ratio of corresponding heights). Example: cone of volume 5 litres cut one-third from vertex → small cone has r = R/3 and h = H/3; volume of small cone = (1/27) × volume of whole cone = 5000/27 ml ≈ 185ml; frustum = 5000 − 185 ≈ 4815ml.

sections.heading: Proof: Angle Sum of a Triangle = 180°
sections.content: Given any triangle ABC, produce BC to X and draw CP parallel to BA. Then: angle BAC = angle ACE (alternate angles, BA ∥ CP); angle ABC = angle ECD (corresponding angles, BA ∥ CP); but angle ACB + angle ACE + angle ECD = 180° (angles on a straight line). Therefore angle BAC + angle ABC + angle ACB = 180°.

sections.heading: Exterior Angle Theorem
sections.content: The exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles (remote interior angles). This follows from the proof: exterior angle = sum of the two alternate and corresponding pairs that equal the two interior angles. Example: for triangle with angles 3x and 2x + 20, and exterior angle 200° − 4x: 3x + 2x + 20 = 200 − 4x → 9x = 180 → x = 20°.
